notes: Eval 2 (fp-ts-checkout) passes 100% in both configurations — the model already has strong fp-ts knowledge. Skill adds depth (before/after comparison table, apS vs bind explanation, TE.chainW) but no assertion-level gap here.
Eval 1 (mutation-refactor) is the strongest differentiator: skill version adds Readonly<T>/readonly annotations and mentions toSorted() — both missing from the baseline.

Eval 3 (closure-loop-bug): both pass all assertions. Skill response cites rule by name (closure-loop-pitfall), explicitly labels Array.from as 'preferred functional style', and omits the legacy IIFE pattern. Baseline suggests IIFE as a valid option.
Skill adds ~16s and ~2500 tokens on average due to reading rule files. Worth it for eval 1 where the quality gap is clear.
